core_mechanism: a discontinuous connectivity (percolation) transition induced by a local competitive selection rule among candidate links, in which biasing each merge toward smaller clusters delays and then abruptly triggers the emergence of a giant connected component at a critical threshold, tractable via mean-field rate/evolution equations for the cluster-size distribution
brief_reason: The abstract describes a graph-evolution process where at each step one edge is chosen from a small competing set sharing common vertices, producing a discontinuous emergence of the giant component analyzed via a system of differential equations, which is exactly a choice-driven discontinuous percolation transition with mean-field cluster-size rate equations.
